intro: Calonemorchis roei, also known as the Robiquetia or Calanthe, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been associated with various health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal infections. The bioactive constituents of Calonemorchis roei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Calonemorchis roei is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures to treat a range of health conditions.
